profiles.detail: the current exceptions profile admits typed throw/catch/rethrow semantics only on the current-host cpu-reference lane and keeps delegate, try_table, and broader proposal-family widening on explicit refusal paths

ir_contract.claim_boundary: this contract names one bounded exception-handling profile over typed throw, catch, and rethrow parity on the current-host cpu-reference lane. It does not claim arbitrary Wasm exception closure, delegate or try_table support, backend-invariant portability, or broader served publication

case_specs.note: typed throw and catch should recover to an exact success state instead of degrading into a generic trap
nested rethrow should preserve typed trap-stack shape instead of collapsing into an undifferentiated failure
tag mismatches should remain trap-parity cases rather than looking like malformed-module refusals
malformed exception handlers should stay explicit refusal truth before execution planning starts

compiler_contract.claim_boundary: this contract freezes one bounded exceptions proposal profile over typed throw, catch, and rethrow semantics. It keeps malformed handlers, delegate, try_table, and broader exception-family widening on explicit refusal paths instead of inheriting them from frozen core-Wasm or generic trap parity
compiler_contract.summary: Exception profile compilation contract freezes 4 cases across 1 success, 2 trap, and 1 refusal expectations.
